# RubyxQube — Owner's Handbook
### Sales, Pricing, Objections & Day-to-Day Ops

---

## PRICING AT A GLANCE

| Plan | Price | What it is |
|---|---|---|
| **Launch** | $1,200 one-time | 5–6 page website, no ongoing support |
| **Autopilot** | $449/mo | Site + AI receptionist + monthly report, 6-month minimum |
| **Momentum** | $999/mo | Everything in Autopilot + GBP management, local SEO, strategy call |
| **AI Chatbot Only** | $500 + $199/mo | Add chatbot to an existing site |
| **Extra Page** | $250 | Beyond the standard build |
| **Copywriting** | $500 | Full site copy, conversion-focused |
| **Logo & Brand Kit** | $350 | Logo + color palette + fonts |
| **Booking Integration** | $150 | Cal.com wired into the site |
| **Domain Setup** | $75 | Register + configure DNS |
| **Business Email** | $100 setup | Google Workspace, you@yourbusiness.com |
| **Site Migration** | $500 | Move from Wix, Squarespace, etc. |

---

## SALES FAQ — COMMON QUESTIONS

### "Do you charge by the hour?"
No. Everything is flat-rate. Autopilot and Momentum are monthly retainers — you know exactly what you're paying every month. Launch is a one-time project fee. No hourly billing, no surprise invoices.

### "What are your fees? How do you charge to build a website?"
Depends on what you need:
- **Just a website:** $1,200 one-time (Launch). Done. No monthly fees.
- **Website + AI receptionist + ongoing support:** $449/month (Autopilot). No setup fee — the site build is included. 6-month minimum commitment.
- **All of the above + active Google presence growth:** $999/month (Momentum).

Most clients who want to actually grow choose Autopilot or Momentum. The $1,200 Launch site gets built and handed off — nobody's watching it after that.

### "Why is there a 6-month minimum on Autopilot?"
The site build alone would cost $1,200 standalone. On Autopilot, there's no setup fee — the build is included in the monthly price, amortized over time. The 6-month minimum makes that math work for both sides. After 6 months it's month-to-month with 30-day notice.

### "What if I want to cancel after the site is built?"
After the 6-month minimum: 30-day written notice, done.

Before 6 months: two options — pay an $800 site buyout fee and walk away with the source files, or cancel with no fee and the site comes down. Most clients who want out early take the buyout.

### "Do I own my website?"
Yes — after the 6-month minimum is complete, the source files are yours on request. During the minimum period, you license it while it's hosted on our infrastructure. Your domain is always yours regardless.

### "How long does it take to build?"
Most sites go live in **3–7 business days** after the intake questionnaire is complete. Complex builds (PSW-level with calculators, portals, catalogs) take 2–4 weeks. Set that expectation on the call.

### "Can I see examples?"
Yes — rubyxqube.com/portfolio. Phoenix Stoneworks (pswboise.com) and Sudz Window & Gutter (sudz-boise.vercel.app) are live client sites.

### "What's the AI receptionist?"
A chatbot trained on the client's business — their services, pricing, hours, FAQs, and service area. It lives in the bottom-right corner of their site, answers questions 24/7, captures leads (name, phone, what they need), and fires an instant SMS + email alert to the owner whenever a lead comes in. Powered by Claude (Anthropic).

### "How much does the AI cost extra?"
Nothing extra — it's included in Autopilot and Momentum. If they already have a site, it's $500 setup + $199/mo standalone.

### "What does '60 min of site updates' mean?"
Each month we have 60 minutes banked for changes — update a price, swap a photo, add a service, fix a typo. Client emails or texts the request, we handle it within 1 business day. Anything beyond 60 min is billed at $150/hr or quoted as a project.

### "I already have a website. Can you just do the AI part?"
Yes — $500 setup + $199/mo. We retrofit the chatbot onto their existing site.

### "Can you work with businesses outside of Boise?"
Yes, fully remote. Treasure Valley is the focus for in-person but we work with service businesses anywhere in the US.

### "Is there a contract?"
Yes — a plain-English service agreement that covers what's included, billing terms, cancellation, and site ownership. Not a scary document. Signed digitally.

### "What if I don't like the site after it's built?"
Two revision rounds are included for all plans. Major direction changes beyond that are scoped and quoted. This almost never happens because we do an intake call first and align on the design before building.

### "Why not just use Wix or Squarespace?"
Those are fine for a basic presence. The difference: our sites are custom-built and fast (Wix/Squarespace carry heavy bloat), include an AI receptionist that actively captures leads, and come with someone watching the site every month. A Wix site at $25/mo gets you a template and no support. Autopilot at $449/mo gets you a custom site, an AI that works while you sleep, and monthly reporting on what's actually happening.

### "Can I add more pages later?"
Yes — $250 per page, quoted per project.

---

## OBJECTION HANDLING

### "That's too expensive."
"Compared to what? A yellow pages ad was $300/mo and you had no idea if it worked. This is $449/mo for a custom site, an AI that captures leads at 2am, and a monthly report showing exactly what's happening. Most clients get ROI from the first booked job."

### "I need to think about it / talk to my partner."
"Totally fair. Can I ask — is it the price, the timing, or something about the service that you want to think through? I'd rather address a real question now than have you sit on it."

### "I can get a website for $500."
"You can get a template for $500. What you're paying for here isn't the HTML — it's the AI receptionist, the lead capture, the SEO setup, and someone actively managing it every month. Those $500 sites get built and forgotten."

### "I'll just use my nephew / a friend who does websites."
"That works for a lot of people. The difference is the AI receptionist and the ongoing support — your nephew builds it and moves on. We're here every month. If you ever want to see what that looks like, the free audit is a good place to start."

### "I'm not sure I need a new website right now."
"Let me run the free audit first — takes 15 minutes and costs nothing. If your site is actually fine, I'll tell you that. Most of the time there are 2-3 specific things costing you leads that are easy to fix."

---

## OPS — DAY-TO-DAY

### Client requests a change
1. Acknowledge within 4 business hours ("Got it, I'll handle this in your next update cycle")
2. Log the request (Notion client page)
3. Batch into the monthly 60-min window unless urgent
4. Complete and confirm with a screenshot
5. Note time used against the 60-min allotment

### Client misses a payment
1. Day 1: Friendly reminder — "Hey, looks like your payment didn't go through, can you update the card?"
2. Day 7: Follow up — same tone, note that service may pause
3. Day 14: Pause updates and chatbot monitoring (not the site) until resolved
4. Day 30: If still unresolved, send formal notice per contract

### Client wants to cancel
1. Ask why — this is data. Sometimes it's fixable (they think they're not getting value, fix the reporting).
2. If they're firm: send written cancellation confirmation, note their 30-day notice period
3. Check if they're within 6-month minimum — if yes, present the buyout option ($800 for files) or no-fee walkaway
4. After 30 days: revoke access, archive their project, export their data

### Monthly report cycle (1st of each month)
1. Pull GA4 data for the previous month (Sessions, users, top pages, traffic source)
2. Pull GSC data (impressions, clicks, top queries)
3. Pull chatbot data (total conversations, leads captured, common questions)
4. Run report-writer agent with that data
5. Send to client by the 5th of the month

### Chatbot is down / not responding
1. Check Vercel function logs (Vercel dashboard → project → Functions tab)
2. Check ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is still valid (not expired or rate-limited)
3. Check NTFY/TextBelt/Resend env vars are present
4. Test with a fresh conversation — if it loads but gives errors, it's usually the API key

### Client asks for something not in scope
"That's outside the monthly update window but easy to quote — it'd be [X] as a one-time add-on. Want me to send a quick proposal?"

### New client onboarding checklist
- [ ] Signed agreement received
- [ ] 50% deposit collected
- [ ] Intake questionnaire sent and completed
- [ ] Domain confirmed (client-owned or we register it)
- [ ] Scaffold new Vercel project
- [ ] Set up client Notion page
- [ ] Build site, test on mobile
- [ ] Set up chatbot + all 3 alert channels (SMS, email, ntfy)
- [ ] Test 10 chatbot conversations
- [ ] Run pre-launch checklist (SOP.md)
- [ ] Collect final 50% payment
- [ ] Launch + send launch email
- [ ] Ask for Google review at peak happiness (launch day)

---

## PRICING PHILOSOPHY

- **Never negotiate down on price.** Offer to scope down instead ("We can start with Launch at $1,200 if you want to test it, then move to Autopilot after you see results").
- **Don't apologize for pricing.** $449/mo is less than one booked job for most service businesses.
- **The free audit is the funnel.** Get them on the call. Once they see a specific problem with their current site, the sale is easier.
- **Founding client discount** — 50% off setup fee for the first 3 clients in exchange for testimonial + case study + Google review. Only if they're a genuinely good fit.

---

## EDGE CASES

### "Can I pause my subscription?"
Not in the standard agreement. Offer a 1-month pause max in extenuating circumstances (medical, seasonal business shutdown). Any pause over 30 days triggers the mid-build kill fee if a build is in progress.

### Client wants a feature that requires a new third-party service
Quote it as a one-time setup fee + any ongoing cost passed through at cost. Never absorb third-party costs without charging for them.

### Client's business closes
Treat as a cancellation. If within the 6-month minimum, apply the standard buyout/walkaway terms. No special exceptions — the contract is the contract.

### Client is unhappy with the site
Don't get defensive. Ask specifically what's wrong. 90% of "I don't like it" issues are fixable in an hour. If it's a genuine mismatch from the intake, offer one additional free revision round. Document it.

### Prospect asks for a proposal before committing to the audit call
Send the standard proposal template — it shows professionalism and answers most questions. But always try to get the call first.
